title: sonchus spinosus var. cervicornis
intro: Sonchus spinosus var. cervicornis, also known as the spineless sow thistle, is a plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sonchus spinosus var. cervicornis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Sonchus spinosus var. cervicornis is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
